threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a flat white and look baffled when it comes in a tiny cup, like you were expecting a venti-sized mug. Classic rookie mistake.
Caught the N73 last night and the driver was belting out old school garage tunes. Made the 3am journey feel like a party, even with all the drunks.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a wild rooster strutting down Richardstraße at 8 AM, like it owned the place. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a bar hidden behind a kebab shop, where the cocktails are named after protest slogans. It's a wild mix of culture and nightlife!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local if they can name the taco truck that’s always parked on 24th and Mission, but can’t find Civic Center on a map.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 today at Civic Center, and the driver had a full on debate with a pigeon at the stop. It won, obviously. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just Gaudí, but really it's the hum of locals at the plaça and the chaos of the festa major that define us.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at the xurros stand, trying to figure out if churros come with chocolate or just sugar.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when someone asks if you want ‘a pint’ instead of a ‘beer’ at the pub on Kingsland Road.
Last night on the 47, a guy serenaded the bus with a Whitney song. Most passengers pretended not to notice but a little girl was loving it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy in flip-flops arguing with a pigeon over a sandwich near the Tempelhofer Feld. That's when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a bar with a rotating selection of street art on the walls, changing every couple of weeks. It's like drinking in a gallery.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re a local when they raise an eyebrow at tourists snapping pics of the mural but don’t bat an eye at a guy juggling on the corner.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 71 yesterday, driver stopped to let a pigeon cross. Whole bus just sat there, not a peep. Just an average day in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is only about the big plazas, but the real magic is in the tiny, hidden calles with old murals and secret bars.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new by their unwavering enthusiasm for pa amb tomàquet—local advice: it’s just bread and tomato, not magic.
